retry_reason: Allocation failure (e.g., -ENOMEM) is not a refcount condition; retrying without freeing memory is unlikely to succeed and is not a standard retry pattern for refcount wrappers.
contract_summary: Set wrapper: allocates a new iommufd object with initial reference count (set via _iommufd_object_alloc) and stores it both in ucmd->new_obj and as the return value. The caller obtains a reference to the object and must eventually call the commit (which transfers the reference) or abort (which releases the reference). The caller must check the return value for errors and not reuse the object after abort.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to allocate a new iommufd object with initial refcounts and store it in ucmd->new_obj. Only minimal validation and assignment logic exists alongside the core allocation/refcount initialization.

check.reason: The returned new_obj from _iommufd_object_alloc has its refcount fields users.refs and wait_cnt.refs initialized via set operations. The access paths start from the return value (new_obj) and correctly trace to the atomic_t fields with matching type chains.
